Stress Testing

The goal of stress testing is to understand how a system behaves under extreme conditions. These conditions include very high loads and simulated failure events.

Monitoring

The application below is used to monitor the status of stress tests in near real time. It provides many different views of metrics generated by the system in addition to data generated by workload processes. When the test completes various reports are available in order to identify potential problems.

Failure Simulations

The graph below describes the various states of a distributed system during a failure and recovery test. The test simulates a failure of 3 out of 6 nodes to determine how the failures affect an active workload.

Resource Usage

Monitoring resource usage is an important aspect of stress testing. The graphs on the left indicate a resource leak associated with database tables. After the problem was fixed, the graphs on the right indicate that the resource leaks no longer occur.
